name: Oklahoma Cornmeal Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the dry ingredients: Shift together 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour, 3/4 cup cornmeal, 1 tablespoon baking powder, 1/4 teaspoon baking soda, 1 teaspoon sugar, and 1 teaspoon salt.
2. Cut in shortening: Add 2 tablespoons of shortening to the dry ingredients. Work it in until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Mix egg and buttermilk: Stir in the beaten egg and 1 cup of buttermilk until the dry ingredients are just moistened.
4. Form dough: Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead the dough lightly, about 3 or 4 times.
5. Roll out dough: Roll the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch. Cut into 2 1/2-inch circles using a round cutter.
6. Fold rolls: Make a crease across each circle and fold one half over. Gently press the edges together to seal.
7. Prepare baking sheet: Place the folded rolls on a greased baking sheet.
8. Bake: Bake at 450 degrees Fahrenheit for 15 minutes, or until lightly browned.
